title: Introduction and Research Context
objective: Establish the scope of the review covering pedestrian crossing behavior research, including both autonomous vehicle interaction and signalized intersection violations, and identify the comparative analysis focus

title: Virtual Reality Approaches to AV-Pedestrian Communication
objective: Synthesize VR-based experimental findings on external communication features for autonomous vehicles and their effects on pedestrian crossing behavior and receptivity

title: Regression Methods for Pedestrian Violation Analysis
objective: Compare statistical modeling approaches for pedestrian red-light running behavior, examining binary logistic regression versus random parameter logit models

title: Cross-Paper Claim Comparison and Synthesis
objective: Evaluate conflicting findings across related studies on pedestrian crossing behavior, identifying where claims converge or diverge and proposing clearer comparison criteria

title: Methodological Limitations and Research Gaps
objective: Synthesize methodological limitations across VR experiments and regression-based studies, highlighting gaps in sample sizes, geographical coverage, and analytical approaches

title: Conclusions and Future Research Directions
objective: Summarize key findings, address the verified gap regarding comparison criteria, and propose future research directions for pedestrian crossing behavior studies
